preamble: The lowering of a high back nasalized vowel [ũ] to a low nasalized vowel [ã] is a significant shift in vowel height and potentially backness, often driven by the acoustic effects of nasalization which tend to lower the perceived height of high vowels.

examples.note: Occurs in various historical developments where nasalized high vowels were lowered significantly over time.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
